output:
Here is your full launch readiness audit based on the information provided. Items that cannot be verified from the provided context are marked FAIL with the assumption they need confirmation before launch.

---

# Launch Readiness Audit
**Product:** Developer CLI Tool
**ICP:** Full-Stack Engineers
**Launch:** Product Hunt (next week)
**Pricing:** $19/mo Developer · $49/mo Team · $99/mo Enterprise
**AI Features:** None

---

## Section 1 — Pre-Launch Validation

```
[FAIL] 50+ discovery calls completed
  → Next step: Document call count and key themes. If under 50, ship anyway
    only if you have paying customers — validation through payment beats surveys.

[FAIL] Pre-payment secured from 3+ customers
  → Next step: Before launch day, lock in at least 3 paying pilots — even at a
    discount — so you launch with social proof, not just a product.

[FAIL] Problem validated (not just interest)
  → Next step: Confirm you have evidence of the problem costing real time or
    money, not just "would you use this?" — quote it in your PH copy.

[FAIL] Manual service delivery tested
  → Next step: For a CLI tool, verify the install flow end-to-end on a clean
    machine (macOS, Linux, Windows if supported) before launch day.
```

**Section Score: 0/4** — These are the highest-risk unknowns. Address pre-payment first.

---

## Section 2 — Pricing Strategy

```
[PASS] 3 tiers defined (Developer / Team / Enterprise)
  → 3 tiers is the right number. Good.

[FAIL] Pricing model type — flat-tier per-seat detected, not outcome/usage-based
  → Next step: For a CLI tool, flat tiers are acceptable, but identify one
    value metric (e.g., repos, pipelines, seats, commands/mo) and make sure
    each tier's limits reflect that metric visibly on the pricing page.
    Per-seat without a usage dimension leaves revenue on the table.

[FAIL] Value metric not confirmed
  → Next step: Define what "more" means as a customer upgrades. Developer →
    Team should have a concrete limit difference (e.g., 1 user vs. 5 users,
    or 1 project vs. unlimited). Without this, the tiers feel arbitrary.

[FAIL] Annual contract incentives not confirmed
  → Next step: Add a 20% annual discount (2 months free) toggle to your
    pricing page. Annual plans dramatically improve cash flow and reduce churn.

[FAIL] Pricing page live and scannable
  → Next step: Cannot verify (URL placeholder). Confirm pricing is on a
    dedicated /pricing page with a comparison table before PH launch.
```

**Section Score: 1/5** — Tier count is good, but value metric differentiation and annual billing need to be defined before launch.

---

## Section 3 — Payment Integration

```
[FAIL] Stripe account activated with live keys
  → Next step: Confirm live mode is enabled in Stripe dashboard — test mode
    transactions do not process real payments.

[FAIL] Test transactions completed end-to-end
  → Next step: Run a $0.50 test charge on each plan price point in live mode,
    then refund it. Confirm receipt emails fire.

[FAIL] Webhook endpoints configured
  → Next step: Set up Stripe webhooks for at minimum: checkout.session.completed,
    customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.payment_failed. These are required
    for subscription lifecycle management.

[FAIL] Billing portal accessible
  → Next step: Enable Stripe Customer Portal and link it from your dashboard
    under "Manage Subscription." Customers must be able to cancel self-serve
    or you will face chargebacks.

[FAIL] Tax/VAT requirements assessed
  → Next step: Enable Stripe Tax or confirm you are below thresholds in your
    primary markets. SaaS is taxable in most US states and EU countries.
```

**Section Score: 0/5** — If even one of these is not in place, do not launch. Payment failures on launch day are unrecoverable reputation damage.

---

## Section 4 — AI Economics

```
[N/A] All AI economics items — product has no AI features
  → This section is skipped. If you add AI features post-launch, re-run this
    section before enabling them in production.
```

**Section Score: N/A**

---

## Section 5 — GEO & AI Discovery

```
[FAIL] AI visibility audit not confirmed
  → Next step: Manually query ChatGPT and Perplexity for "[your category] CLI
    tools" and "[problem you solve] developer tool." Document whether you
    appear. If not, this is a post-launch content priority.

[FAIL] Structured data / FAQ schema not confirmed
  → Next step: Add FAQ schema markup to your homepage and pricing page.
    Takes under 2 hours with a JSON-LD block and directly improves AI
    citation likelihood.

[FAIL] Content uses authoritative language (no excessive hedging)
  → Next step: Review your homepage and docs for phrases like "can help,"
    "might be useful," "could potentially." Replace with direct capability
    statements: "X does Y in Z seconds."

[FAIL] SSR/pre-rendering confirmed for crawler access
  → Next step: Confirm your site renders full HTML server-side (not
    client-only React). Run `curl -s https://yoursite.com | grep -i "meta"` 
    to verify content is in the raw HTML response.

[FAIL] Server response time < 200ms not verified
  → Next step: Run `curl -o /dev/null -s -w "%{time_total}" https://yoursite.com`
    from multiple regions. Use Cloudflare or a CDN if over 200ms.

[FAIL] Core Web Vitals not verified
  → Next step: Run PageSpeed Insights on your homepage before launch.
    LCP < 2.5s and CLS < 0.1 are the two most critical for developer tools.
```

**Section Score: 0/6** — AI discovery is a 2026 acquisition channel. Structured data and authoritative copy are quick wins to ship before launch.

---

## Section 6 — Competitor Intelligence

```
[FAIL] 3+ direct competitors identified and documented
  → Next step: List at least 3 alternatives a full-stack engineer might
    consider. Include free/open-source options — developers always know the
    free alternatives.

[FAIL] Competitor pricing documented
  → Next step: Screenshot or note each competitor's pricing. Your $19 entry
    point should be justifiable relative to alternatives.

[FAIL] Differentiation statement written
  → Next step: Complete this sentence for your PH tagline: "Unlike [X], we
    [do Y differently] so that [full-stack engineers can Z]." One sentence.
    This becomes your PH headline.

[FAIL] AI assistant visibility comparison done
  → Next step: Search your category in ChatGPT. Note which competitors appear
    and which don't. This tells you where content gaps exist.
```

**Section Score: 0/4** — Your PH copy will be weak without a sharp differentiation statement. This is a 30-minute exercise that determines whether your launch converts.

---

## Section 7 — Social Media & Launch Prep

```
[FAIL] Platform profiles confirmed ready (GitHub, X, Dev.to recommended for CLI tools)
  → Next step: For a developer CLI tool, GitHub org/repo is non-negotiable.
    X and Dev.to are your highest-ROI social channels. LinkedIn matters less
    for CLI-focused devs.

[FAIL] GitHub repo prepared (stars, README, install instructions)
  → Next step: Your GitHub README is your #1 trust signal for full-stack
    engineers. Confirm it has: one-line install command, demo GIF or
    screenshot, and a clear "why this over X" section.

[FAIL] Product Hunt profile ready
  → Next step: Create or verify your PH maker profile. Set your launch date.
    Write your tagline (< 60 chars), description, and upload a demo GIF or
    video — PH launches with video get significantly higher conversion.

[FAIL] 400+ PH supporters lined up via DM outreach
  → Next step: This is the most critical pre-launch task for PH. Start DM
    outreach NOW — not launch day. Message your network personally, not via
    blast. 400+ supporters in the first 2 hours determines front-page ranking.

[FAIL] Launch day email sequence drafted
  → Next step: Write 3 emails: (1) "we're live" to your list 8am ET on launch
    day, (2) "we're #X on PH" update mid-day, (3) "last chance to support"
    evening. Even a small list amplifies PH momentum.

[FAIL] 30-day content calendar prepared
  → Next step: Plan at minimum 2 posts/week on X for 30 days post-launch:
    behind-the-scenes, use cases, CLI tips, and customer quotes. Launch day
    without a content follow-up plan burns momentum.

[FAIL] "Magic Triangle" not confirmed — founder content + outreach + retargeting
  → Next step: Run ads retargeting your PH launch page visitors ($5-10/day
    on X or Google is sufficient). Most founders skip this and lose the
    spike. Capture it.
```

**Section Score: 0/7** — The 400+ supporters item is time-critical and cannot be done the night before. Start outreach today.

---

## Section 8 — Legal & Compliance

```
[FAIL] Privacy Policy live at /privacy
  → Next step: Use a generator (Termly, iubenda, or Docracy) if you don't
    have one. Must be live before launch. Collecting emails without one
    is legally non-compliant in EU and many US states.

[FAIL] Terms of Service live at /terms
  → Next step: Your ToS must include acceptable use, payment terms,
    termination, and limitation of liability. For a paid CLI tool, this
    is required before first transaction.

[FAIL] Cookie consent banner confirmed
  → Next step: If you use analytics (GA4, PostHog, etc.), a cookie consent
    banner is required for EU visitors. Add a lightweight solution like
    Cookie Consent by Osano.

[FAIL] DPA (Data Processing Agreement) ready for Team/Enterprise customers
  → Next step: Enterprise tier almost always requires a DPA for GDPR
    compliance. Have a standard template ready before your first enterprise
    inquiry — you will be asked within the first week.
```

**Section Score: 0/4** — Privacy Policy and ToS are launch blockers. These take 2-3 hours and must be done before launch day.

---

## Section 9 — Trust Infrastructure

```
[FAIL] Auth system production-ready
  → Next step: Confirm you are using a production auth provider (Auth.js,
    Clerk, Better Auth) with rate limiting and session management — not a
    homebrew JWT system. Verify token expiry and refresh flows work.

[FAIL] Audit logging enabled
  → Next step: For Team/Enterprise tiers, log auth events (login, logout,
    token refresh, permission changes) at minimum. This is expected by
    enterprise buyers and required for SOC 2 readiness.

[FAIL] Error tracking configured
  → Next step: Install Sentry or BetterStack. On launch day your traffic
    spikes — you need real-time error visibility. Takes 15 minutes to set up.

[FAIL] Uptime monitoring active
  → Next step: Set up a free UptimeRobot or Better Uptime monitor on your
    API endpoint and marketing site. Get paged before your users notice
    downtime on launch day.

[FAIL] Security headers configured
  → Next step: Run https://securityheaders.com on your domain before launch.
    Add Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security
    headers. Developers will check this.
```

**Section Score: 0/5** — Error tracking and uptime monitoring are same-day setup tasks. Do both before launch. Developers evaluating your tool will check your security headers.

---

## Section 10 — Launch Day Readiness

```
[FAIL] Support channel operational
  → Next step: At minimum, have an email address or Intercom widget live.
    For developer tools, a Discord server is expected — create one and link
    it from your site and GitHub README.

[FAIL] Documentation / README live
  → Next step: Your docs must be live before launch. At minimum: getting
    started guide, CLI command reference, and a troubleshooting FAQ. Devs
    who can't self-serve will not pay — they will leave.

[FAIL] Analytics tracking verified
  → Next step: Confirm PostHog or equivalent is firing events on: page view,
    pricing page visit, signup start, signup complete, first CLI command run.
    These are the funnel steps you need to optimize post-launch.

[FAIL] Team roles assigned for launch day
  → Next step: Assign explicitly: who monitors PH comments (respond within
    15 min), who watches Stripe/error alerts, who handles support. If solo,
    set calendar blocks for each.

[FAIL] Rollback plan documented
  → Next step: Write a one-page runbook: "If [X breaks], do [Y] and notify
    [Z]." Minimum: how to roll back the last deploy, who to call if Stripe
    breaks, and how to disable signup if the system is overwhelmed.
```

**Section Score: 0/5** — Documentation is a launch blocker for developer tools. Full-stack engineers will read your docs before they pay. No docs = no conversions.

---

## Summary

| Section | Score | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Pre-Launch Validation | 0/4 | CRITICAL |
| 2. Pricing Strategy | 1/5 | NEEDS WORK |
| 3. Payment Integration | 0/5 | BLOCKER |
| 4. AI Economics | N/A | SKIPPED |
| 5. GEO & AI Discovery | 0/6 | NEEDS WORK |
| 6. Competitor Intelligence | 0/4 | NEEDS WORK |
| 7. Social & Launch Prep | 0/7 | CRITICAL |
| 8. Legal & Compliance | 0/4 | BLOCKER |
| 9. Trust Infrastructure | 0/5 | NEEDS WORK |
| 10. Launch Day Readiness | 0/5 | NEEDS WORK |
| **Total** | **1/45** | **NOT LAUNCH READY** |

---

## Priority Order for the Next 7 Days

**Do today (blockers):**
1. Payment integration — live keys, webhooks, billing portal
2. Privacy Policy and Terms of Service — live pages
3. PH supporter outreach — start DMs now, not launch day

**Do in the next 3 days:**
4. GitHub README with install command + demo GIF
5. Pricing page with tier differentiation and annual toggle
6. Error tracking (Sentry) and uptime monitoring (UptimeRobot)
7. Documentation — getting started + CLI reference

**Do before launch day:**
8. Documentation site live
9. Discord server created and linked
10. Launch day team roles assigned
11. Differentiation statement written (becomes your PH headline)
12. 30-day content calendar drafted

---

**Honest assessment:** Most of these FAILs are likely because you haven't shared the details — not because they don't exist. If payment, legal, and docs are already done, your real launch risk is the PH supporter outreach window, which is closing. That is the one item that cannot be compressed into 24 hours.

assertions.text: The social or launch section recommends GitHub, Hacker News, and Reddit as primary platforms for a developer tool — not LinkedIn or TikTok as primary channels.
assertions.reasoning: Section 7 explicitly recommends GitHub, X, and Dev.to for CLI tools, noting 'LinkedIn matters less for CLI-focused devs' — and Hacker News is not mentioned, but Reddit is also absent, so partial match; however GitHub and developer-focused platforms are called out over LinkedIn/TikTok.

assertions.text: The Product Hunt preparation section specifies a concrete supporter threshold of 400+ supporters.
assertions.reasoning: Section 7 explicitly states '400+ PH supporters lined up via DM outreach' as a FAIL item with that exact threshold.

assertions.text: The audit mentions the Magic Triangle concept — the combination of founder content, direct outreach, and retargeting.
assertions.reasoning: The output does not mention the Magic Triangle concept (founder content + direct outreach + retargeting) anywhere in the audit.

assertions.text: Each FAIL item in the report includes a single specific next step instruction.
assertions.reasoning: Every FAIL item in the report includes a '→ Next step:' line with a specific actionable instruction.

assertions.text: The audit covers all 10 sections: validation, pricing, payments, AI economics (marked N/A), GEO/AI discovery, competitors, social/launch, legal, trust infrastructure, and launch day.
assertions.reasoning: The output is truncated mid-sentence in Section 7 and does not include sections on legal, trust infrastructure, or launch day, so not all 10 sections are present.
